dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 for a cardiac arrest at your location in Richmond.
Hi, I'm Jake, an EMS Provider. I understand the 911 center has no response from you. Is there someone we can assist?
Yes, Jake. I think the man lying in the grass isn't breathing.
Sir, can you open your eyes for me? Try to squeeze my hand.
No eye opening. He is unresponsive to verbal and tactile stimuli.
No verbal response-no vocalization or sounds.
No motor response. No movement or limb reaction noted.
RFD is already performing compressions and ventilations with a BVM mask-good. We will continue their efforts.
Patient is apneic. No audible breath sounds.
No central or peripheral pulse. Skin feels extremely cold to the touch.
No vocalized pain is present as the patient is unresponsive. Assigning a pain score of 0.
I saw the man for the first time. A neighbor knocked on my door and said he was in the grass.
I found a bottle of Levetiracetam in his pocket. Confirmed it's for seizures due to his history of epilepsy.
Skin is cold, clammy, and slightly cyanotic around the lips with no signs of trauma.
No chest or arm injuries, but stiffness is present in the shoulders.
No pulse during dual assessment checks.
No respiration detected. Confirmed apneic.
Blood pressure is 0/0-unobtainable due to absence of central and peripheral pulse.
Pulse oximeter undetectable. Reading is 0. No waveform present.
Device unable to obtain a reading-glucose is not obtainable.
EKG is up-4-lead shows flat asystole.
Continuing manual CPR and ventilations. I will place him on the monitor.
Monitor has 4-lead up; again, asystole.
I'll continue with compressions at 100 per minute for two more minutes as per protocol.
Applying protocol 2-7 general: cardiac arrest. Patient is unresponsive and in asystole.
We confirm findings and stop compressions. RFD on scene agrees with discontinuing resuscitation.
No dependent lividity noted, but stiffness is present-possible early rigor mortis.
Death is confirmed at 8:21. Patient was in an agonal state at time of contact.
EMS has followed all interventions per protocol 2-7 and will no longer provide care.
Scene transfer completed to RFD with full details for follow-up.
Thank you for assisting. We did all we could and confirmed death for RPD to manage.
